contexts.audience: Young Men's Lyceum of Springfield and a civic public concerned with republican institutions
contexts.occasion: Early public address on mob violence, political inheritance, and the perpetuation of American institutions
contexts.rhetorical_action: Warns that republican survival depends on civic discipline and reverence for law.

agency_structure.displacement_mechanism: National survival is framed as collective self-preservation rather than as conflict among named social groups.

contexts.evidence_span: As a nation of freemen we must live through all time, or die by suicide.
contexts.claim_boundary: Genre-sensitive interpretation; do not aggregate as frequency without document/register context.

contexts.audience: Cemetery dedication audience, Union public, mourners, soldiers, and national readers
contexts.occasion: Dedication of the national cemetery after the Battle of Gettysburg
contexts.rhetorical_action: Transforms battlefield death into renewed obligation to preserve democratic government and freedom.

agency_structure.displacement_mechanism: Deaths are generalized as national offering and obligation; specific agents and material bodies recede behind public dedication.

contexts.evidence_span: We have come to dedicate a portion of that field as a final resting place for those who here gave their lives that this nation might live.
contexts.claim_boundary: Genre-sensitive interpretation; do not aggregate as frequency without document/register context.

contexts.audience: Second inaugural audience, wartime public, Union and returning Confederate publics
contexts.occasion: Second presidential inauguration near the end of the Civil War
contexts.rhetorical_action: Reframes the war as providential judgment on slavery and redirects victory toward reconciliation.

agency_structure.displacement_mechanism: Human agency is partially displaced into providential judgment and shared national guilt.
